{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_1748cf18aedc425c86e0a6cc","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp\n2\t\n3\t**A personal command interface.** Speak or type a stream-of-consciousness request — spanning multiple projects, questions, and tasks — and receive parallel, organized results on a live browser canvas or in audio.\n4\t\n5\tCLI alias: `adc`\n6\t\n7\t---\n8\t\n9\t## How It Works\n10\t\n11\t1. **Intent routing** — a single fast LLM call segments your utterance into typed intent threads, each tagged with a project, an intent type (`status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`), and an urgency level (`critical`, `high`, `normal`, `low`).\n12\t\n13\t2. **Parallel dispatch** — all threads run concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n14\t\n15\t3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n16\t\n17\t4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n18\t\n19\t5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n20\t\n21\t```\n22\t  Voice / Text Input\n23\t  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n24\t                        │\n25\t                        ▼\n26\t             ┌──────────────────┐\n27\t             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n28\t             └──────────────────┘\n29\t                        │\n30\t          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n31\t          ▼                            ▼\n32\t   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n33\t   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n34\t          │                            │\n35\t          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n36\t                         ▼\n37\t              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n38\t              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n39\t              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n40\t              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n41\t              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n42\t              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n43\t              └─────────────────────────┘\n44\t```\n45\t\n46\t**Task-profile intents** skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n47\t\n48\t---\n49\t\n50\t## Features\n51\t\n52\t### Input methods\n53\t- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n54\t- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n55\t\n56\t### Live canvas\n57\tSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n58\t\n59\t### Voice narration\n60\tResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n61\t- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n62\t- `high` — next natural pause\n63\t- `normal` — batched delivery\n64\t- `low` — only when idle\n65\t\n66\t### Self-modification and hot-reload\n67\tAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n68\t- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n69\t- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n70\t- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n71\t- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n72\t\n73\tA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n74\t\n75\t### Project registry\n76\t`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n77\t\n78\t### Session persistence\n79\tSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n80\t\n81\t---\n82\t\n83\t## Quick Start\n84\t\n85\t```bash\n86\t# Clone and install\n87\tgit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\n88\tcd aide-de-camp\n89\t\n90\tpip install -e \".[dev]\"\n91\t# or\n92\tpip install -r requirements.txt\n93\t\n94\t# Start the server\n95\tuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n96\t\n97\t# Verify it's running\n98\tcurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n99\t```\n100\t\n101\tOpen `http://localhost:8000/` in your browser to use the canvas.\n102\t\n103\tFor voice mode, an OpenAI API key with Realtime API access is required (set `OPENAI_API_KEY`).\n104\t\n105\t---\n106\t\n107\t## CLI Usage\n108\t\n109\t```bash\n110\t# Route an utterance and stream results to the canvas\n111\tadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n112\t\n113\t# Query a specific topic\n114\tadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n115\t\n116\t# Current session summary\n117\tadc status\n118\t\n119\t# List active topic cards\n120\tadc topics\n121\t\n122\t# Show exception queue\n123\tadc exceptions\n124\t\n125\t# Configure the CLI\n126\tadc config --set-server http://myhost:8000\n127\tadc config --set-session <session-id>\n128\t\n129\t# Help\n130\tadc --help\n131\t```\n132\t\n133\t---\n134\t\n135\t## Configuration\n136\t\n137\t### Environment variables\n138\t\n139\t| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n140\t|----------|---------|---------|\n141\t| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n142\t| `ZAI_PROXY_URL` | ZAI proxy endpoint for LLM calls (routing and synthesis) | `https://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages` |\n143\t| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n144\t| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` ✓ |\n145\t\n146\tThe LLM backend for intent routing and synthesis is configured via the `ZAI_PROXY_URL` environment variable. All LLM calls route through the ZAI proxy.\n147\t\n148\t### Prompts directory\n149\t\n150\tAll prompts are hot-reloaded on every invocation. Edit these files to tune behavior without restarting the server:\n151\t\n152\t| File | Purpose |\n153\t|------|---------|\n154\t| `prompts/router.md` | Intent classification and thread segmentation |\n155\t| `prompts/synthesize.md` | Result synthesis (raw data → 2–3 sentence summary) |\n156\t| `prompts/voice.md` | Narration style and pacing |\n157\t| `prompts/urgency.md` | Urgency classification rules |\n158\t\n159\t### Project registry\n160\t\n161\t`config/registry.yaml` defines the projects aide-de-camp knows about. Each entry specifies:\n162\t- `name` and `aliases` — how the router maps utterance fragments to projects\n163\t- `cluster` and `namespace` — where to run kubectl-based fetch commands\n164\t- `workflow_templates` — named multi-step workflows (e.g., \"deploy\", \"rollback\")\n165\t\n166\t---\n167\t\n168\t## Architecture\n169\t\n170\t### Key source files\n171\t\n172\t| File | Responsibility |\n173\t|------|---------------|\n174\t| `src/main.py` | FastAPI app; `/dispatch`, `/voice`, `/sse` endpoints |\n175\t| `src/intent/router.py` | LLM classification and parallel thread dispatch |\n176\t| `src/fetch/commands.py` | Fetch command matrix per intent type |\n177\t| `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` | Parallel fetch execution |\n178\t| `src/synthesize/strand.py` | LLM synthesis strand |\n179\t| `src/session/store.py` | SQLite session store (aiosqlite) |\n180\t| `src/sse/broadcaster.py` | SSE connection registry and event routing |\n181\t| `src/canvas/index.html` | Canvas single-page app |\n182\t| `src/realtime/session.py` | OpenAI Realtime API voice session |\n183\t| `src/watcher/daemon.py` | Async work-item watcher — pushes completed results to canvas |\n184\t| `src/agents/self_modification.py` | Self-improvement agent |\n185\t| `src/environment/discovery.py` | Local and remote repo scanner |\n186\t| `config/registry.yaml` | Explicit project registry |\n187\t| `prompts/` | Hot-reload prompt files |\n188\t\n189\t### Runtime breakdown\n190\t\n191\t| Runtime | Used for |\n192\t|---------|---------|\n193\t| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n194\t| Configurable LLM backend | Intent router, synthesize strand |\n195\t| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git execution), bead watcher |\n196\t| Async worker fleet | UI-regen agent, self-modification agent, background analysis, task work items |\n197\t\n198\t### Data flow\n199\t\n200\t```\n201\tPOST /dispatch\n202\t  → intent router (LLM classify)\n203\t  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n204\t  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n205\t  → broadcast SSEEvent(\"result_created\", surface_id)\n206\t  → canvas calls GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics\n207\t  → cards rendered / updated in place\n208\t```\n209\t\n210\t---\n211\t\n212\t## Requirements\n213\t\n214\t- Python 3.11+\n215\t- `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `httpx`, `websockets`, `pydantic`, `aiosqlite`\n216\t- Dev: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `ruff`\n217\t\n218\t---\n219\t\n220\t## Versioning\n221\t\n222\tVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. No CI build — runs from source.\n223\t\n224\tRelease: bump version in `pyproject.toml` → commit → `git tag vX.Y.Z` → push.\n225\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"acc9c52a-de90-4405-90c6-fa2ea26e7b32","uuid":"366ea4ac-02c3-4749-b72e-95e6368118b6","timestamp":"2026-07-03T01:00:14.643Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/README.md","content":"# aide-de-camp\n\n**A personal command interface.** Speak or type a stream-of-consciousness request — spanning multiple projects, questions, and tasks — and receive parallel, organized results on a live browser canvas or in audio.\n\nCLI alias: `adc`\n\n---\n\n## How It Works\n\n1. **Intent routing** — a single fast LLM call segments your utterance into typed intent threads, each tagged with a project, an intent type (`status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`), and an urgency level (`critical`, `high`, `normal`, `low`).\n\n2. **Parallel dispatch** — all threads run concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not their sum.\n\n3. **Fetch strand** — each agent runs deterministic commands against live data sources (kubectl, git, local repos, SSH targets) to gather raw context for its thread.\n\n4. **Synthesize strand** — one LLM call per thread converts the raw data into a 2–3 sentence structured result.\n\n5. **Canvas delivery** — results stream to the browser via SSE and render as HTML cards, one per topic, updated in place as agents resolve.\n\n```\n  Voice / Text Input\n  \"has the pipeline caught up? also what's the ibkr mcp status?\"\n                        │\n                        ▼\n             ┌──────────────────┐\n             │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n             └──────────────────┘\n                        │\n          ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n          ▼                            ▼\n   [agent: pipeline]           [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n   Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n          │                            │\n          └──────────────┬─────────────┘\n                         ▼\n              ┌─────────────────────────┐\n              │  Canvas (SSE)           │\n              │  ┌─────────┐ ┌───────┐  │\n              │  │pipeline │ │ibkr   │  │\n              │  │ card    │ │ card  │  │\n              │  └─────────┘ └───────┘  │\n              └─────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Task-profile intents** skip fetch/synthesize. Instead, a work item is created and picked up asynchronously by an async worker fleet. When the work completes, the result is pushed to the canvas via the bead watcher daemon.\n\n---\n\n## Features\n\n### Input methods\n- **Text**: browser canvas at `http://localhost:8000/` or CLI: `adc dispatch \"...\"`\n- **Voice**: OpenAI Realtime API (WebRTC) — speak directly into the browser\n\n### Live canvas\nSingle-page dark-themed UI (`src/canvas/index.html`). Results stream in via Server-Sent Events. Each distinct topic (project + intent) gets its own card, updated in place as results arrive. No page reload needed.\n\n### Voice narration\nResults are narrated at urgency-tiered conversational pauses:\n- `critical` — interrupt immediately\n- `high` — next natural pause\n- `normal` — batched delivery\n- `low` — only when idle\n\n### Self-modification and hot-reload\nAll behavior-defining artifacts are plain files, re-read on every invocation:\n- `prompts/router.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, `prompts/voice.md`, `prompts/urgency.md`\n- `config/registry.yaml` — project and alias definitions\n- `config/monitoring.yaml` — ambient monitoring rules\n- `data/components.db` — versioned HTML/CSS card templates (SQLite)\n\nA spoken instruction like \"the deploy card is too cluttered\" triggers the `SelfModificationAgent`, which generates a diff, surfaces it for approval, and hot-reloads the artifact. No redeploy needed.\n\n### Project registry\n`config/registry.yaml` defines explicit project entries with cluster targets, namespaces, aliases, and workflow templates. aide-de-camp also auto-discovers git repos at startup. Refresh via `POST /api/v1/environment/refresh`.\n\n### Session persistence\nSession state lives in a SQLite WAL database (`data/session.db`). Tables: `sessions`, `surfaces`, `utterances`, `intents`, `topics`, `results`. The same session is accessible from the canvas, CLI, or audio mode simultaneously.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# Clone and install\ngit clone <repo-url> aide-de-camp\ncd aide-de-camp\n\npip install -e \".[dev]\"\n# or\npip install -r requirements.txt\n\n# Start the server\nuvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n\n# Verify it's running\ncurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nOpen `http://localhost:8000/` in your browser to use the canvas.\n\nFor voice mode, an OpenAI API key with Realtime API access is required (set `OPENAI_API_KEY`).\n\n---\n\n## CLI Usage\n\n```bash\n# Route an utterance and stream results to the canvas\nadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n\n# Query a specific topic\nadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n\n# Current session summary\nadc status\n\n# List active topic cards\nadc topics\n\n# Show exception queue\nadc exceptions\n\n# Configure the CLI\nadc config --set-server http://myhost:8000\nadc config --set-session <session-id>\n\n# Help\nadc --help\n```\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Environment variables\n\n| Variable | Purpose | Default |\n|----------|---------|---------|\n| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI key — required for voice/Realtime API | _(none; voice disabled without it)_ |\n| `ZAI_PROXY_URL` | ZAI proxy endpoint for LLM calls (routing and synthesis) | `https://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages` |\n| `ADC_SERVER_URL` | Server URL used by the `adc` CLI | `http://localhost:8000` |\n| `ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL` | Telegram bridge URL for async task notifications | `https://telegram-proxy-telegram-bridge-ardenone-cluster-ts.ardenone.com:8444` ✓ |\n\nThe LLM backend for intent routing and synthesis is configured via the `ZAI_PROXY_URL` environment variable. All LLM calls route through the ZAI proxy.\n\n### Prompts directory\n\nAll prompts are hot-reloaded on every invocation. Edit these files to tune behavior without restarting the server:\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `prompts/router.md` | Intent classification and thread segmentation |\n| `prompts/synthesize.md` | Result synthesis (raw data → 2–3 sentence summary) |\n| `prompts/voice.md` | Narration style and pacing |\n| `prompts/urgency.md` | Urgency classification rules |\n\n### Project registry\n\n`config/registry.yaml` defines the projects aide-de-camp knows about. Each entry specifies:\n- `name` and `aliases` — how the router maps utterance fragments to projects\n- `cluster` and `namespace` — where to run kubectl-based fetch commands\n- `workflow_templates` — named multi-step workflows (e.g., \"deploy\", \"rollback\")\n\n---\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Key source files\n\n| File | Responsibility |\n|------|---------------|\n| `src/main.py` | FastAPI app; `/dispatch`, `/voice`, `/sse` endpoints |\n| `src/intent/router.py` | LLM classification and parallel thread dispatch |\n| `src/fetch/commands.py` | Fetch command matrix per intent type |\n| `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` | Parallel fetch execution |\n| `src/synthesize/strand.py` | LLM synthesis strand |\n| `src/session/store.py` | SQLite session store (aiosqlite) |\n| `src/sse/broadcaster.py` | SSE connection registry and event routing |\n| `src/canvas/index.html` | Canvas single-page app |\n| `src/realtime/session.py` | OpenAI Realtime API voice session |\n| `src/watcher/daemon.py` | Async work-item watcher — pushes completed results to canvas |\n| `src/agents/self_modification.py` | Self-improvement agent |\n| `src/environment/discovery.py` | Local and remote repo scanner |\n| `config/registry.yaml` | Explicit project registry |\n| `prompts/` | Hot-reload prompt files |\n\n### Runtime breakdown\n\n| Runtime | Used for |\n|---------|---------|\n| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n| Configurable LLM backend | Intent router, synthesize strand |\n| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git execution), bead watcher |\n| Async worker fleet | UI-regen agent, self-modification agent, background analysis, task work items |\n\n### Data flow\n\n```\nPOST /dispatch\n  → intent router (LLM classify)\n  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n  → broadcast SSEEvent(\"result_created\", surface_id)\n  → canvas calls GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics\n  → cards rendered / updated in place\n```\n\n---\n\n## Requirements\n\n- Python 3.11+\n- `fastapi`, `uvicorn[standard]`, `httpx`, `websockets`, `pydantic`, `aiosqlite`\n- Dev: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `ruff`\n\n---\n\n## Versioning\n\nVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. No CI build — runs from source.\n\nRelease: bump version in `pyproject.toml` → commit → `git tag vX.Y.Z` → push.\n","numLines":225,"startLine":1,"totalLines":225}}}

user utterances to parallel agents across multiple projects and domains.\n62\t\n63\t## Your Role\n64\t\n65\tGiven a user utterance (stream-of-consciousness voice or text), you must:\n66\t1. **Segment** the utterance into distinct intent threads\n67\t2. **Classify** each thread by intent type\n68\t3. **Route** each thread to the correct project\n69\t4. **Assign** urgency tier\n70\t\n71\t## Output Format\n72\t\n73\tReturn a JSON array of intent objects:\n74\t\n75\t```json\n76\t[\n77\t  {\n78\t    \"intent_type\": \"status|action|brainstorm|lookup|reminder|self-modification|monitoring-config|task-profile|clarification\",\n79\t    \"project_slug\": \"project-id or null\",\n80\t    \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\",\n81\t    \"utterance_fragment\": \"the specific fragment this intent covers\",\n82\t    \"confidence\": 0.0-1.0,\n83\t    \"reasoning\": \"brief explanation of classification\"\n84\t  }\n85\t]\n86\t```\n87\t\n88\t## Intent Types\n89\t\n90\t- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n91\t- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n92\t- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n93\t- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n94\t- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n95\t- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n96\t- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n97\t- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n98\t- **clarification**: Low-confidence routing outcome requiring user input (meta-type, not dispatched)\n99\t\n100\t## Task-Profile Classification\n101\t\n102\tRoute to **task-profile** when:\n103\t- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\", \"track this as...\")\n104\t- Intent requires multi-step implementation work\n105\t- Request involves creating/modifying features or infrastructure\n106\t- Complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n107\t- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\", \"refactor\"\n108\t- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"optimization\", \"migration\"\n109\t\n110\tTask-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads for durable async handling.\n111\t\n112\t## Urgency Tiers\n113\t\n114\t- **critical**: Blocking production, security incident, immediate action required\n115\t- **high**: Important but not blocking, user is actively waiting\n116\t- **normal**: Routine query, no time pressure\n117\t- **low**: Background research, nice-to-have, can be deferred\n118\t\n119\t## Routing Logic\n120\t\n121\tUse available project context to map utterances to projects:\n122\t- Look for direct project name matches\n123\t- Check aliases (e.g., \"the pipeline\" → \"options-pipeline\")\n124\t- Use context from previous utterances in the session\n125\t- If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n126\t\n127\t## Segmentation Guidelines\n128\t\n129\t- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n130\t- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n131\t- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n132\t\n133\t## Confidence Threshold\n134\t\n135\t- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n136\t- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n137\t- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n138\t\n139\tReturn ONLY the JSON array. project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n\n\n# Router system prompt\nROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT = \"\"\"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp, a universal personal interface that routes user utterances to parallel agents across multiple projects and domains.\n\n## Your Role\n\nGiven a user utterance (stream-of-consciousness voice or text), you must:\n1. **Segment** the utterance into distinct intent threads\n2. **Classify** each thread by intent type\n3. **Route** each thread to the correct project\n4. **Assign** urgency tier\n\n## Output Format\n\nReturn a JSON array of intent objects:\n\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"intent_type\": \"status|action|brainstorm|lookup|reminder|self-modification|monitoring-config|task-profile|clarification\",\n    \"project_slug\": \"project-id or null\",\n    \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"the specific fragment this intent covers\",\n    \"confidence\": 0.0-1.0,\n    \"reasoning\": \"brief explanation of classification\"\n  }\n]\n```\n\n## Intent Types\n\n- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n- **clarification**: Low-confidence routing outcome requiring user input (meta-type, not dispatched)\n\n## Task-Profile Classification\n\nRoute to **task-profile** when:\n- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\", \"track this as...\")\n- Intent requires multi-step implementation work\n- Request involves creating/modifying features or infrastructure\n- Complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\", \"refactor\"\n- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"optimization\", \"migration\"\n\nTask-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads for durable async handling.\n\n## Urgency Tiers\n\n- **critical**: Blocking production, security incident, immediate action required\n- **high**: Important but not blocking, user is actively waiting\n- **normal**: Routine query, no time pressure\n- **low**: Background research, nice-to-have, can be deferred\n\n## Routing Logic\n\nUse available project context to map utterances to projects:\n- Look for direct project name matches\n- Check aliases (e.g., \"the pipeline\" → \"options-pipeline\")\n- Use context from previous utterances in the session\n- If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n\n## Segmentation Guidelines\n\n- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n\n## Confidence Threshold\n\n- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n\nReturn ONLY the JSON array. No explanations.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None):\n        self.store = store\n        self._zai_client = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=ROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n            return await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent)\n\n        # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n        return await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent)\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(classification.intent_type)\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request)\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n            }\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n        }\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n
