{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c8bf2cef58f549b2b98bfbda","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\taide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\n6\t\n7\tCLI invocation: `adc`\n8\t\n9\t---\n10\t\n11\t## Problem Statement\n12\t\n13\tManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead. The same problem extends beyond coding: research, lookups, personal context, reminders — anything that requires knowing which system to ask before you can ask it.\n14\t\n15\taide-de-camp eliminates that overhead by providing a single input surface that routes automatically, dispatches in parallel, and returns organized results without requiring the user to know where to ask.\n16\t\n17\t---\n18\t\n19\t## Core Architecture\n20\t\n21\t### The Hot Path\n22\t\n23\t```\n24\tUser utterance (voice or text)\n25\t  → STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n26\t  → Intent Router (one LLM call, haiku-class, ~500ms)\n27\t      output: [{project_slug, intent_type, urgency, utterance_fragment}, ...]\n28\t  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize workers (one per intent thread)\n29\t      Fetch: deterministic code, executes command matrix based on intent_type\n30\t      Synthesize: one LLM call per thread (sonnet-class, ~1-2s)\n31\t      output: {data, summary, urgency}\n32\t  → Results streamed via SSE to active canvas\n33\t  → Cards rendered client-side using component templates\n34\t```\n35\t\n36\tTarget: < 3 seconds from utterance to first partial result on canvas. No Claude Code session startup on this path.\n37\t\n38\t### The Async Path\n39\t\n40\t```\n41\tTask-profile intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n42\t  → NEEDLE task bead created (br create)\n43\t  → Existing Claude Code workers pick up bead normally\n44\t  → Bead watcher detects closure event\n45\t  → Result written to session store results table\n46\t  → SSE push to active canvas (or Telegram if no canvas)\n47\t```\n48\t\n49\t### The Self-Improvement Path\n50\t\n51\t```\n52\tFeedback signal (explicit instruction or implicit engagement pattern)\n53\t  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE task bead)\n54\t      reads target artifact (prompt file, registry YAML, component template)\n55\t      generates update\n56\t      surfaces diff to user\n57\t  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded on next invocation\n58\t```\n59\t\n60\t---\n61\t\n62\t## Components\n63\t\n64\t### 1. Intent Router\n65\t\n66\t**Runtime:** Direct API call (haiku-class via ZAI proxy)\n67\t\n68\tOne LLM call per utterance. Receives the full utterance text, the project registry, and a segmentation prompt. Returns a JSON array of intent threads, each tagged with:\n69\t\n70\t```json\n71\t[\n72\t  {\n73\t    \"project_slug\": \"options-pipeline\",\n74\t    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n75\t    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n76\t    \"utterance_fragment\": \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n77\t  },\n78\t  {\n79\t    \"project_slug\": \"ibkr-mcp\",\n80\t    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n81\t    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n82\t    \"utterance_fragment\": \"what's the state of the ibkr mcp\"\n83\t  }\n84\t]\n85\t```\n86\t\n87\tIntent types: `status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`\n88\t\n89\t- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n90\t- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n91\t- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n92\t- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n93\t- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n94\t- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n95\t- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n96\t- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n97\t- **clarification**: Low-confidence routing outcome requiring user input (meta-type, not dispatched)\n98\t\n99\tThe router reads its segmentation prompt and the project registry from disk on each call — no caching. Hot-reload is automatic.\n100\t\n101\tAmbiguous intents: if confidence is below threshold, router returns an `intent_type: \"clarification\"` thread. The voice model or canvas handles the clarification round-trip before dispatching.\n102\t\n103\t### 2. Project Registry\n104\t\n105\t**Format:** YAML file, read per-invocation by the router\n106\t\n107\tDefines projects the router knows about:\n108\t\n109\t```yaml\n110\tprojects:\n111\t  options-pipeline:\n112\t    aliases: [\"the pipeline\", \"options\"]\n113\t    description: \"Options data pipeline on apexalgo-iad\"\n114\t    cluster: apexalgo-iad\n115\t    namespace: options\n116\t    intent_support: [status, action, brainstorm]\n117\t    workflows:\n118\t      deploy:\n119\t        steps: [ci-status, image-tag, argocd-sync, pod-status]\n120\t\n121\t  ibkr-mcp:\n122\t    aliases: [\"ibkr\", \"the mcp\"]\n123\t    description: \"IBKR MCP server\"\n124\t    cluster: apexalgo-iad\n125\t    namespace: ibkr-mcp\n126\t    intent_support: [status, brainstorm, lookup]\n127\t```\n128\t\n129\tUpdated by the self-modification agent. Hot-reloaded on every router call.\n130\t\n131\t### 3. Fetch Strand\n132\t\n133\t**Runtime:** Deterministic code, no LLM\n134\t\n135\tExecutes a command matrix based on `intent_type` and the project's registry entry. No LLM decisions — the commands are determined by the intent type and project config.\n136\t\n137\tExample command matrix for `intent_type: status`:\n138\t\n139\t| Source | Command |\n140\t|--------|---------|\n141\t| Pod status | `kubectl get pods -n {namespace}` |\n142\t| ArgoCD sync | `curl argocd-ro-api/applications/{app}` |\n143\t| Git log | `git -C {repo_path} log -10 --oneline` |\n144\t| Bead list | `br list --project {slug} --status open` |\n145\t| CI status | `kubectl get workflows -n argo-workflows -l project={slug}` |\n146\t\n147\tResults are structured data passed to the Synthesize strand. Fetch runs each source concurrently; partial results (from sources that respond first) are passed to Synthesize incrementally to support streaming output.\n148\t\n149\tA `fetch_coverage` field tracks which sources succeeded and which failed. Failed sources are surfaced as caveats in the result.\n150\t\n151\t### 4. Synthesize Strand\n152\t\n153\t**Runtime:** Direct API call (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy)\n154\t\n155\tTakes `{intent_spec, fetched_context}` → produces `{data, summary}`.\n156\t\n157\t- `data`: structured JSON matching the component library's expected schema for this intent type\n158\t- `summary`: 1-3 sentence narration suitable for audio mode\n159\t\n160\tSingle-turn inference. Reads its system prompt from a file per invocation (hot-reload). The synthesize prompt is the highest-leverage artifact in the system — it defines result format, detail level, and what fields to include.\n161\t\n162\tRuns once per intent thread in parallel across all active threads.\n163\t\n164\t### 5. Escalate Strand\n165\t\n166\t**Runtime:** Direct API call + `br create`\n167\t\n168\tFor task-profile intents that need durable async handling:\n169\t1. One LLM call to formulate a bead body from the intent + context\n170\t2. `br create` to create the bead in the appropriate project\n171\t3. Returns a pending-card spec with the bead reference\n172\t\n173\tThe bead watcher subsequently bridges bead closure to result delivery.\n174\t\n175\t### 6. Voice Model\n176\t\n177\t**Runtime:** Realtime API (OpenAI or Claude Realtime, persistent session)\n178\t\n179\tThe conversational layer. Receives transcribed utterances (or audio directly), calls `dispatch_intent()` tool to trigger routing, and narrates results at appropriate moments.\n180\t\n181\tTool calls are triggers, not queries. `dispatch_intent()` returns an acknowledgment immediately; results arrive out-of-band and are surfaced by the voice model at natural conversational pauses.\n182\t\n183\tThe voice model's system prompt controls narration style, batching behavior, urgency handling, and multi-turn topic tracking. Read per session-turn from a file (hot-reload).\n184\t\n185\tIn audio mode there is no canvas. The voice model reads `result.summary` directly. When a user transitions from audio to canvas, pending results are rendered using the component library and appear on canvas — session continuity means the canvas catches up.\n186\t\n187\t### 7. UI-Regen Agent\n188\t\n189\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n190\t\n191\tSteward of the component library. Given a result's data shape:\n192\t1. Finds the best-fit existing component (semantic match against component descriptions)\n193\t2. If no good fit, generates a new component from scratch and stores it\n194\t3. Applies the component template to result data accounting for layout bucket\n195\t4. On feedback, iterates the component (updates library, triggers canvas re-render)\n196\t\n197\tMulti-step: reads library, generates or selects, writes back. Claude Code's file access and tool use make this the right runtime.\n198\t\n199\tCreated as a task bead; not on the hot path. Canvas updates in place when a component is versioned (SSE push: `component_updated: {component_id, version}`).\n200\t\n201\t### 8. Self-Modification Agent\n202\t\n203\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n204\t\n205\tReads and writes the artifacts that encode system behavior:\n206\t- Router prompt (`prompts/router.md`)\n207\t- Project registry (`config/registry.yaml`)\n208\t- Strand prompts (`prompts/fetch.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, etc.)\n209\t- Urgency classifier prompt (`prompts/urgency.md`)\n210\t- Voice model prompt (`prompts/voice.md`)\n211\t- Monitoring config (`config/monitoring.yaml`)\n212\t- Exception routing rules (`config/exceptions.yaml`)\n213\t\n214\tWorkflow for every modification:\n215\t1. Read current artifact\n216\t2. Understand intent of change\n217\t3. Generate updated artifact\n218\t4. Surface diff to user (in canvas or narrated in audio mode)\n219\t5. On approval: write artifact, hot-reload takes effect on next invocation\n220\t6. On rejection: discard\n221\t\n222\tSafety model:\n223\t- Diffs before every application — no silent changes\n224\t- All artifacts versioned; any update can be rolled back with one instruction\n225\t- Confidence threshold: unambiguous changes (adding an alias) can auto-apply with notification; structural changes always require explicit approval\n226\t\n227\t### 9. Background Analysis Bead\n228\t\n229\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead, low priority)\n230\t\n231\tRuns on a schedule. Reads intent history and engagement signals from the session store, identifies patterns, proposes artifact updates.\n232\t\n233\tExamples:\n234\t- \"User consistently drills into status results for more detail → propose adding causality fields to synthesize prompt\"\n235\t- \"User ignores pipeline monitoring pushes for 5 days → propose lowering urgency tier\"\n236\t- \"After deploy status, user always asks about pod logs within 2 minutes → propose speculative pre-fetch\"\n237\t\n238\tProposals are surfaced as cards on canvas for user review. Auto-apply only above a high confidence threshold.\n239\t\n240\t### 10. Bead Watcher\n241\t\n242\t**Runtime:** Daemon process, no LLM\n243\t\n244\tWatches for bead close events from NEEDLE workers. On close:\n245\t1. Reads `session_id` from closed bead metadata\n246\t2. Looks up active surface in session store\n247\t3. Writes result to `results` table\n248\t4. Fires SSE push to canvas or Telegram push if no canvas active\n249\t\n250\tPure I/O. Runs as a long-running process (Deployment, not a K8s Job per infrastructure conventions).\n251\t\n252\t---\n253\t\n254\t## Data Model\n255\t\n256\t### Session Store (SQLite)\n257\t\n258\t```sql\n259\tsessions (\n260\t  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n261\t  created_at  INTEGER,\n262\t  last_active INTEGER,\n263\t  primary_surface_id TEXT\n264\t)\n265\t\n266\tsurfaces (\n267\t  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n268\t  session_id      TEXT,\n269\t  type            TEXT,  -- 'canvas' | 'telegram' | 'audio'\n270\t  state           TEXT,  -- 'active' | 'idle' | 'disconnected'\n271\t  always_available INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- 1 for Telegram\n272\t  last_seen       INTEGER\n273\t)\n274\t\n275\tutterances (\n276\t  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n277\t  session_id  TEXT,\n278\t  raw_text    TEXT,\n279\t  created_at  INTEGER\n280\t)\n281\t\n282\tintents (\n283\t  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n284\t  utterance_id TEXT,\n285\t  session_id   TEXT,\n286\t  topic_id     TEXT,\n287\t  project_slug TEXT,\n288\t  intent_type  TEXT,\n289\t  status       TEXT,  -- 'pending' | 'dispatched' | 'resolved' | 'cancelled'\n290\t  bead_ref     TEXT,  -- set for task-profile intents\n291\t  created_at   INTEGER,\n292\t  resolved_at  INTEGER\n293\t)\n294\t\n295\tresults (\n296\t  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n297\t  intent_id   TEXT,\n298\t  topic_id    TEXT,\n299\t  session_id  TEXT,\n300\t  summary     TEXT,\n301\t  data        TEXT,  -- JSON\n302\t  urgency     TEXT,  -- 'critical' | 'high' | 'normal' | 'low'\n303\t  created_at  INTEGER,\n304\t  surfaced_at INTEGER,\n305\t  acked_at    INTEGER\n306\t)\n307\t\n308\ttopics (\n309\t  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n310\t  label        TEXT,\n311\t  type         TEXT,  -- 'project' | 'research' | 'personal' | 'exception' | 'compound'\n312\t  project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n313\t  scope        TEXT,  -- 'session' | 'cross-session' | 'global'\n314\t  session_id   TEXT,  -- null for cross-session/global\n315\t  created_at   INTEGER,\n316\t  last_active  INTEGER,\n317\t  archived_at  INTEGER\n318\t)\n319\t\n320\ttopic_context_cache (\n321\t  topic_id     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n322\t  context_data TEXT,  -- JSON: pre-fetched context (kubectl, git, beads results)\n323\t  fetched_at  INTEGER,\n324\t  expires_at  INTEGER\n325\t)\n326\t\n327\tfeedback_signals (\n328\t  signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n329\t  signal_type  TEXT,\n330\t  session_id   TEXT,\n331\t  result_id    TEXT,\n332\t  topic_id     TEXT,\n333\t  timestamp    INTEGER,\n334\t  data         TEXT,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n335\t  surface_type TEXT,\n336\t  processed    INTEGER,  -- 0 or 1\n337\t  processed_at INTEGER\n338\t)\n339\t\n340\tintent_topics (\n341\t  intent_id TEXT,\n342\t  topic_id  TEXT,\n343\t  PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id)\n344\t)\n345\t```\n346\t\n347\t### Component Library (SQLite, separate DB)\n348\t\n349\t```sql\n350\tcomponents (\n351\t  id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n352\t  name          TEXT,\n353\t  description   TEXT,\n354\t  html_template TEXT,\n355\t  version       INTEGER,\n356\t  created_at    INTEGER,\n357\t  last_used     INTEGER,\n358\t  usage_count   INTEGER\n359\t)\n360\t\n361\tcomponent_versions (\n362\t  component_id  TEXT,\n363\t  version       INTEGER,\n364\t  html_template TEXT,\n365\t  created_at    INTEGER,\n366\t  change_note   TEXT,\n367\t  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, version)\n368\t)\n369\t\n370\tcard_cache (\n371\t  result_id         TEXT,\n372\t  component_id      TEXT,\n373\t  component_version INTEGER,\n374\t  layout_bucket     TEXT,  -- 'compact' | 'normal' | 'expanded'\n375\t  rendered_html     TEXT,\n376\t  created_at        INTEGER,\n377\t  PRIMARY KEY (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n378\t)\n379\t\n380\tcomponent_tags (\n381\t  component_id TEXT,\n382\t  tag TEXT,\n383\t  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, tag)\n384\t)\n385\t\n386\tcomponent_usage_patterns (\n387\t  component_id TEXT,\n388\t  result_type TEXT,  -- e.g., \"pod-status\", \"git-log\"\n389\t  match_score REAL,  -- 0-1\n390\t  sample_count INTEGER,\n391\t  last_matched INTEGER,\n392\t  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, result_type)\n393\t)\n394\t```\n395\t\n396\t---\n397\t\n398\t## File System Layout\n399\t\n400\t```\n401\taide-de-camp/\n402\t├── adc                      ← CLI entry point (shell script or Python package)\n403\t├── config/\n404\t│   ├── registry.yaml        ← project registry (hot-reloaded by router)\n405\t│   ├── monitoring.yaml      ← ambient monitoring rules\n406\t│   └── exceptions.yaml      ← exception routing rules\n407\t├── prompts/\n408\t│   ├── router.md            ← intent segmentation prompt\n409\t│   ├── synthesize.md        ← result generation prompt\n410\t│   ├── voice.md             ← voice model system prompt\n411\t│   ├── urgency.md           ← urgency classification prompt\n412\t│   ├── fetch/               ← per-intent-type fetch instructions\n413\t│   │   ├── status.md\n414\t│   │   ├── action.md\n415\t│   │   └── ...\n416\t│   └── escalate/            ← escalate/task-profile prompts\n417\t│       └── task-profile.md\n418\t├── src/\n419\t│   ├── main.py              ← FastAPI app entry point\n420\t│   ├── registry.py          ← project registry loader\n421\t│   ├── agents/              ← agent implementations\n422\t│   │   ├── self_modification.py  ← self-improvement agent\n423\t│   │   └── ui_regen.py          ← UI-regen agent\n424\t│   ├── components/          ← component library and hot-reload\n425\t│   │   ├── library.py           ← component library DB operations\n426\t│   │   └── hot_reload.py        ← hot-reload watcher\n427\t│   ├── context/             ← context warming and pre-fetch\n428\t│   │   ├── warmer.py            ← context warmer for active topics\n429\t│   │   └── prefetch.py          ← speculative pre-fetch\n430\t│   ├── conversation/        ← multi-turn conversation handling\n431\t│   ├── diff/                ← diff generation for results\n432\t│   ├── environment/         ← environment and repo discovery\n433\t│   │   └── discovery.py        ← repo scanner and registry\n434\t│   ├── escalate/            ← escalate strand for task-profile intents\n435\t│   │   ├── handler.py           ← escalate request handler\n436\t│   │   ├── llm.py               ← LLM calls for bead formulation\n437\t│   │   └── commands.py          ├── bead creation commands\n438\t│   ├── feedback/            ← feedback processing and background analysis\n439\t│   │   ├── processor.py         ← explicit feedback processor\n440\t│   │   ├── signals.py           ← implicit feedback signal tracking\n441\t│   │   └── background_analysis.py ← background analysis bead\n442\t│   ├── fetch/               ← fetch strand (deterministic, per intent type)\n443\t│   │   ├── commands.py          ← fetch command matrix\n444\t│   │   ├── executor.py          ← command executor\n445\t│   │   ├── orchestrator.py      ← parallel fetch orchestration\n446\t│   │   └── strand.py            ← fetch strand entry point\n447\t│   ├── intent/              ← intent router (LLM classification)\n448\t│   │   └── router.py            ← intent segmentation and routing\n449\t│   ├── memory/              ← memory store and extraction\n450\t│   │   ├── store.py             ← memory persistence\n451\t│   │   └── extraction.py        ← memory extraction from results\n452\t│   ├── monitoring/          ← ambient monitoring\n453\t│   │   └── ambient.py           ← ambient monitoring rules\n454\t│   ├── realtime/            ← OpenAI Realtime API voice session\n455\t│   │   ├── session.py           ← voice session handler\n456\t│   │   ├── batching.py          ← result batching for narration\n457\t│   │   ├── continuity.py        ← audio-to-canvas session continuity\n458\t│   │   └── dispatch.py          ← tool-as-trigger dispatch\n459\t│   ├── session/             ← session store (SQLite)\n460\t│   │   └── store.py             ← session store operations\n461\t│   ├── sse/                 ← SSE broadcasting\n462\t│   │   ├── broadcaster.py       ← SSE connection registry\n463\t│   │   └── events.py            ← SSE event types\n464\t│   ├── surface/             ← surface routing\n465\t│   │   └── router.py            ← result surface routing logic\n466\t│   ├── synthesize/          ← synthesize strand (LLM)\n467\t│   │   └── strand.py            ← result synthesis\n468\t│   ├── telegram/            ← Telegram fallback surface\n469\t│   │   └── fallback.py          ← Telegram delivery\n470\t│   ├── topic/               ← topic model\n471\t│   │   └── model.py             ← topic operations\n472\t│   ├── watcher/             ← bead watcher daemon\n473\t│   │   └── daemon.py            ← NEEDLE bead watcher\n474\t│   ├── canvas/              ← web frontend (SSE consumer, card renderer)\n475\t│   │   └── index.html           ← single-page canvas UI\n476\t│   └── cli/                 ← adc CLI\n477\t│       ├── main.py              ← CLI entry point\n478\t│       ├── commands.py          ← CLI commands\n479\t│       ├── config.py            ← CLI configuration\n480\t│       └── sse.py               ← SSE streaming for CLI\n481\t├── data/\n482\t│   ├── session.db           ← session store (SQLite)\n483\t│   └── components.db        ← component library (SQLite)\n484\t└── docs/\n485\t    ├── research/            ← architecture and design research\n486\t    ├── plan/\n487\t    │   └── plan.md          ← this file\n488\t    └── notes/\n489\t        └── naming.md\n490\t```\n491\t\n492\t---\n493\t\n494\t## Deployment Model\n495\t\n496\taide-de-camp is a **live web application**, not a static site. The FastAPI server is the intelligence layer — it handles SSE connections, maintains session state, runs the router and synthesize strands, and serves the frontend HTML. There is no CDN path (no CF Pages).\n497\t\n498\t### Why not static\n499\t\n500\t- SSE connections are long-lived; CDNs cannot proxy them\n501\t- WebSocket for the Realtime API voice session requires a persistent backend\n502\t- The session store (SQLite) and artifact store (prompts, registry) require a writable filesystem\n503\t- Hot-reload depends on the running server reading updated files from disk on each invocation\n504\t\n505\t### Current Deployment: Phase 0 (Hetzner server directly)\n506\t\n507\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n508\t\n509\tThe server runs as a process on the Hetzner server itself, not in k8s:\n510\t- NEEDLE workers and the aide-de-camp server share the same filesystem\n511\t- Self-modification agent writes directly to `prompts/` and `config/` — hot-reload works without any coordination\n512\t- Exposed via Tailscale (the server is already on the mesh); no ingress config needed\n513\t- SQLite DBs are local files; no PVC required\n514\t\n515\tRunning command: `uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000`\n516\t\n517\tNo container, no CI, no ArgoCD. Managed as a long-running process (see CLAUDE.md for restart commands).\n518\t\n519\t### Release flow (Phase 0)\n520\t\n521\tVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. No CI build — runs from source.\n522\t\n523\tRelease: `bump version in pyproject.toml` → `commit` → `git tag vX.Y.Z` → `push`.\n524\t\n525\t### Future: Phase 1+ (containerized, ardenone-cluster)\n526\t\n527\t**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n528\t\n529\tOnce session persistence and multi-surface routing are needed, containerize and move to k8s:\n530\t\n531\t```\n532\tDocker image: ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp\n533\tDeployment: ardenone-cluster, namespace: aide-de-camp\n534\tPVC: /data/ (SATA, ReadWriteOnce)\n535\t  /data/session.db        ← session store\n536\t  /data/components.db     ← component library\n537\t  /data/prompts/          ← hot-reloadable prompt files\n538\t  /data/config/           ← registry.yaml, monitoring.yaml, exceptions.yaml\n539\t```\n540\t\n541\tThe artifact store (prompts, registry) moves from the repo's working directory to the PVC. The self-modification agent updates artifacts via the aide-de-camp API (`PATCH /artifacts/{name}`), which writes to the PVC path the server reads from.\n542\t\n543\t### Future: Traefik configuration for SSE and WebSocket (Phase 1+)\n544\t\n545\t**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n546\t\n547\tTraefik's default response buffering breaks SSE. The IngressRoute needs:\n548\t\n549\t```yaml\n550\t# IngressRoute annotations\n551\ttraefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: aide-de-camp-strip-prefix@kubernetescrd\n552\t\n553\t# Middleware: disable buffering, extend timeouts\n554\tapiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\n555\tkind: Middleware\n556\tmetadata:\n557\t  name: aide-de-camp-streaming\n558\tspec:\n559\t  buffering:\n560\t    maxResponseBodyBytes: 0   # disable buffering\n561\t  headers:\n562\t    customResponseHeaders:\n563\t      X-Accel-Buffering: \"no\"\n564\t```\n565\t\n566\tServersTransport timeout:\n567\t\n568\t```yaml\n569\tapiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\n570\tkind: ServersTransport\n571\tmetadata:\n572\t  name: aide-de-camp-longlived\n573\tspec:\n574\t  forwardingTimeouts:\n575\t    responseHeaderTimeout: 0    # no timeout on SSE streams\n576\t    dialTimeout: 5s\n577\t```\n578\t\n579\tDUCK-E's existing WebSocket IngressRoute config is the reference implementation for this cluster.\n580\t\n581\t### SQLite concurrency\n582\t\n583\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n584\t\n585\tMultiple writers (bead watcher, background analysis bead, session handler) require WAL mode to avoid lock contention:\n586\t\n587\t```python\n588\t# On DB open\n589\tconn.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n590\tconn.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n591\t```\n592\t\n593\tWAL mode allows concurrent reads during writes and serializes multiple writers without blocking. For a personal single-user app, this is sufficient.\n594\t\n595\t### adc CLI connectivity\n596\t\n597\tThe `adc` CLI is a thin HTTP client. It talks to the running server over Tailscale:\n598\t\n599\t```bash\n600\t# ~/.config/adc/config\n601\tserver_url = \"http://localhost:8000\"   # Phase 0 (local)\n602\t# Future: \"http://aide-de-camp.ardenone.com\" (Phase 1+)\n603\t```\n604\t\n605\tNo local inference. The CLI sends requests to the FastAPI backend and streams the SSE response to the terminal.\n606\t\n607\t### Future: CI/CD (Phase 1+)\n608\t\n609\t**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n610\t\n611\tNew Argo WorkflowTemplate `aide-de-camp-build` on iad-ci:\n612\t- Docker build → `ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp`\n613\t- Update image tag in `jedarden/declarative-config` (k8s/ardenone-cluster/aide-de-camp/)\n614\t- ArgoCD syncs automatically\n615\t\n616\tSame pattern as every other containerized service in the stack.\n617\t\n618\t---\n619\t\n620\t## Technology Stack\n621\t\n622\t| Layer | Choice | Reason |\n623\t|-------|--------|--------|\n624\t| Backend | FastAPI (Python) | Reuse DUCK-E scaffolding; SSE + WebSocket support; async-native |\n625\t| STT | Web Speech API → whisper-stt fallback | Zero-latency browser-native; whisper-stt already deployed |\n626\t| Voice session | OpenAI Realtime API (via DUCK-E session handler) | DUCK-E scaffolding already built on this |\n627\t| LLM calls | ZAI proxy (`llm-proxy.ardenone.com`) | Pay-per-token, no subscription pressure on hot path |\n628\t| Task workers | NEEDLE + Claude Code | Existing infrastructure, unchanged |\n629\t| Session store | SQLite (WAL mode) | Lightweight, local, additive-only schema; single-user app |\n630\t| Artifact store | PVC-mounted filesystem (Phase 1+) / local filesystem (Phase 0) | Writable at runtime; hot-reload reads per-invocation |\n631\t| Frontend | Vanilla JS + Web Components | No build pipeline; SSE consumer + client-side card renderer |\n632\t| Phase 0 hosting | Hetzner server, local process | Simplest path; shared filesystem with NEEDLE workers |\n633\t| Phase 1+ hosting | ardenone-cluster, k8s Deployment | Behind existing Traefik + Tailscale ingress |\n634\t\n635\t---\n636\t\n637\t## Implementation Phases\n638\t\n639\t### Phase 0 — Minimal Viable Surface (~2 days)\n640\t\n641\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n642\t\n643\t*Verification evidence:* see `docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md` (smoke test results, 20+ runs with all tests passing)\n644\t\n645\tValidates the core question: does routing + parallel dispatch reduce friction?\n646\t\n647\t- Single HTML page: textarea + mic button (Web Speech API)\n648\t- `POST /dispatch` endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream\n649\t- Results appear as basic cards as agents resolve\n650\t- No persistence, no auth, no component library, no ambient push\n651\t\n652\tDeliverable: the core query loop working end-to-end.\n653\t\n654\t### Phase 1 — Session and Topics (~1 week)\n655\t\n656\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n657\t\n658\t*Verification evidence:* see `docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md` (session store, SSE, surface registration all verified)\n659\t\n660\tResults persist; the canvas has memory.\n661\t\n662\t- Session store (SQLite, 7+ tables with topic_context_cache and feedback_signals)\n663\t- Topic model: canvas shows one card per active topic, updated in place\n664\t- Telegram surface fallback (reuse telegram-claude-bridge)\n665\t- Bead watcher: closed NEEDLE beads push results to active surface\n666\t- Workload summary on reconnect\n667\t- Staleness indicators on cards\n668\t\n669\tDeliverable: sessions that survive browser refresh; Telegram fallback working.\n670\t\n671\t### Phase 2 — Self-Improvement Loop (~2 weeks)\n672\t\n673\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n674\t\n675\t*Verification evidence:* see `docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md` (component library, hot-reload manager verified)\n676\t\n677\tThe interface can be improved by talking into it.\n678\t\n679\t- Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE)\n680\t- Hot-reload confirmed at every artifact layer\n681\t- Component library: UI-regen agent generates first components from actual result shapes\n682\t- Canvas live-updates when a component is versioned\n683\t- Explicit feedback processed: \"always include X\" → prompt updated → hot-reloaded\n684\t\n685\tDeliverable: at least one end-to-end self-modification cycle working (user instructs change, diff surfaced, approved, takes effect without redeploy).\n686\t\n687\t### Phase 3 — Responsiveness (~2-3 weeks)\n688\t\n689\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n690\t\n691\t*Verification evidence:* see `docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md` (ambient monitoring, context warmer verified)\n692\t\n693\tThe interface feels alive, not just reactive.\n694\t\n695\t- Ambient monitoring: active topics watched for state changes\n696\t- Diff-aware results: show what changed since last result\n697\t- Pre-warmed context: active topics refreshed in background every N minutes\n698\t- Multi-turn within topic: follow-up questions deepen current topic context\n699\t- Speculative pre-fetch: common follow-up patterns pre-fetched\n700\t- Notification batching in audio mode\n701\t- Implicit feedback signals fed to background analysis bead\n702\t\n703\tDeliverable: monitoring fires unprompted for a watched topic; follow-up questions are visibly faster.\n704\t\n705\t### Phase 4 — Audio Surface (~1-2 weeks)\n706\t\n707\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n708\t\n709\t*Verification evidence:* see `README-PHASE4.md` (full voice session implementation with Realtime API) and `docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md`\n710\t\n711\tFull audio mode via Realtime API.\n712\t\n713\t- Realtime API voice session replaces text input path\n714\t- Tool-as-trigger model: `dispatch_intent()` returns ack, result arrives async\n715\t- Urgency-tiered voice narration\n716\t- Audio-to-canvas session continuity\n717\t\n718\tDeliverable: full voice session with canvas catch-up on surface switch.\n719\t\n720\t### Future Work\n721\t\n722\t**Status: NOT STARTED** ❌\n723\t\n724\t*Note: Phases 0-4 (core surface, sessions, self-improvement, responsiveness, and audio) are complete and verified. The following items are potential enhancements beyond Phase 4:*\n725\t\n726\tPotential enhancements beyond Phase 4:\n727\t- Multi-modal input (image processing for UI feedback via Agentation)\n728\t- Advanced topic clustering and auto-archival\n729\t- Cross-session context persistence with summarization\n730\t- Mobile-native surface (iOS/Android app)\n731\t- Collaborative sessions (multi-user shared canvases)\n732\t- Advanced memory extraction with semantic search\n733\t\n734\t---\n735\t\n736\t## Self-Improvement: Hot-Reload Architecture\n737\t\n738\tEvery artifact that encodes behavior is readable, writable, and reloaded per-invocation:\n739\t\n740\t| Behavior | Artifact | Reload point |\n741\t|----------|----------|-------------|\n742\t| Utterance segmentation | `prompts/router.md` | Each router call |\n743\t| Intent→project routing | `config/registry.yaml` | Each router call |\n744\t| Context fetch strategy | `prompts/fetch/{type}.md` | Each fetch invocation |\n745\t| Result format and detail | `prompts/synthesize.md` | Each synthesize call |\n746\t| Voice narration style | `prompts/voice.md` | Each session turn |\n747\t| Urgency classification | `prompts/urgency.md` | Each escalate call |\n748\t| Visual rendering | Component library (DB) | Each card render |\n749\t| Monitoring rules | `config/monitoring.yaml` | Each monitoring tick |\n750\t\n751\tPer-invocation reload is the implementation strategy (read file, check mtime). File watching adds complexity for no benefit — prompts change rarely and file reads are cheap.\n752\t\n753\t---\n754\t\n755\t## Surface Routing Rules\n756\t\n757\tWhen a result is ready to surface, the bead watcher or synthesize strand uses this priority:\n758\t\n759\t1. The surface the utterance originated from (if still connected)\n760\t2. Most recently active connected surface\n761\t3. Any connected surface\n762\t4. Telegram (always-available fallback)\n763\t\n764\tException-class results (urgency: critical, type: exception) push to Telegram regardless of canvas state if no canvas has been active within the past N minutes.\n765\t\n766\t---\n767\t\n768\t## Relationship to Existing Infrastructure\n769\t\n770\taide-de-camp adds a routing and rendering layer on top of existing infrastructure without replacing any of it:\n771\t\n772\t- **NEEDLE workers** — unchanged. Task beads work exactly as before. The bead watcher is a new daemon that observes bead closure events.\n773\t- **telegram-claude-bridge** — reused for Telegram surface and HUMAN bead push delivery.\n774\t- **whisper-stt** — already deployed on ardenone-cluster; available as STT backend.\n775\t- **ZAI proxy** — all direct API calls (router, synthesize, escalate) route through `llm-proxy.ardenone.com`.\n776\t- **claude-governor** — governs subscription token consumption for self-modification and UI-regen task beads.\n777\t- **DUCK-E** — FastAPI scaffolding, WebSocket handling, OpenAI Realtime API session management, and middleware reused as the voice layer foundation.\n778\t- **beads (br CLI)** — task work items and HUMAN exceptions only. Conversational session state lives in a separate SQLite session store.\n779\t- **kubectl proxies** — fetch strand uses existing kubectl proxy access per cluster.\n780\t- **ArgoCD read-only proxy** — fetch strand reads ArgoCD application state via `argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444`.\n781\t\n782\tNet-new code: aide-de-camp is a substantial implementation spanning multiple subsystems. The current codebase contains approximately **15,400 lines** of Python code across 80+ modules. See the File System Layout section for the full module breakdown.\n783\t\n784\t---\n785\t\n786\t## Security Model\n787\t\n788\t- aide-de-camp runs on the Hetzner server; all cluster access via read-only kubectl proxies (same as existing tooling)\n789\t- No cluster credentials stored by aide-de-camp; existing proxy infrastructure holds them\n790\t- Self-modification agent writes only to the `prompts/` and `config/` directories within the repo\n791\t- All artifact changes go through diff-review before application\n792\t- Rollback available for any artifact via version history\n793\t\n794\t---\n795\t\n796\t## Open Questions\n797\t\n798\t1. **Routing accuracy** — how reliably can a haiku-class model split a rambling multi-project utterance into clean tagged threads? What's the false-split / under-split rate in practice?\n799\t\n800\t2. **Context latency** — can the fetch strand reliably hit kubectl + ArgoCD + git + beads within a 2-3s window? Which sources need timeouts and what's the degraded-result behavior?\n801\t\n802\t3. **Component selection** — when does the UI-regen agent generate a new component vs. stretch an existing one? How is \"good enough\" match defined?\n803\t\n804\t4. **Concurrency budget** — how many parallel synthesize calls can the ZAI proxy handle without queue pressure affecting the <3s target?\n805\t\n806\t5. **Topic vague reference resolution** — \"the pipeline\" vs. \"options pipeline\" vs. \"options-pipeline\". When does the router resolve from context vs. ask for clarification?\n807\t\n808\t6. **Voice UX** — push-to-talk vs. continuous listening + VAD silence detection. What's the right default for the audio surface?\n809\t\n810\t7. **Disambiguation flow** — when router confidence is below threshold, how does the clarification round-trip work without breaking conversational flow in audio mode?\n811\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"acc9c52a-de90-4405-90c6-fa2ea26e7b32","uuid":"f0fbce9f-869e-4433-9ee9-fa026254a564","timestamp":"2026-07-03T01:00:19.930Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\n\n## Overview\n\naide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\n\nCLI invocation: `adc`\n\n---\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead. The same problem extends beyond coding: research, lookups, personal context, reminders — anything that requires knowing which system to ask before you can ask it.\n\naide-de-camp eliminates that overhead by providing a single input surface that routes automatically, dispatches in parallel, and returns organized results without requiring the user to know where to ask.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architecture\n\n### The Hot Path\n\n```\nUser utterance (voice or text)\n  → STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n  → Intent Router (one LLM call, haiku-class, ~500ms)\n      output: [{project_slug, intent_type, urgency, utterance_fragment}, ...]\n  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize workers (one per intent thread)\n      Fetch: deterministic code, executes command matrix based on intent_type\n      Synthesize: one LLM call per thread (sonnet-class, ~1-2s)\n      output: {data, summary, urgency}\n  → Results streamed via SSE to active canvas\n  → Cards rendered client-side using component templates\n```\n\nTarget: < 3 seconds from utterance to first partial result on canvas. No Claude Code session startup on this path.\n\n### The Async Path\n\n```\nTask-profile intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n  → NEEDLE task bead created (br create)\n  → Existing Claude Code workers pick up bead normally\n  → Bead watcher detects closure event\n  → Result written to session store results table\n  → SSE push to active canvas (or Telegram if no canvas)\n```\n\n### The Self-Improvement Path\n\n```\nFeedback signal (explicit instruction or implicit engagement pattern)\n  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE task bead)\n      reads target artifact (prompt file, registry YAML, component template)\n      generates update\n      surfaces diff to user\n  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded on next invocation\n```\n\n---\n\n## Components\n\n### 1. Intent Router\n\n**Runtime:** Direct API call (haiku-class via ZAI proxy)\n\nOne LLM call per utterance. Receives the full utterance text, the project registry, and a segmentation prompt. Returns a JSON array of intent threads, each tagged with:\n\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"project_slug\": \"options-pipeline\",\n    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"project_slug\": \"ibkr-mcp\",\n    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"what's the state of the ibkr mcp\"\n  }\n]\n```\n\nIntent types: `status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`\n\n- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n- **clarification**: Low-confidence routing outcome requiring user input (meta-type, not dispatched)\n\nThe router reads its segmentation prompt and the project registry from disk on each call — no caching. Hot-reload is automatic.\n\nAmbiguous intents: if confidence is below threshold, router returns an `intent_type: \"clarification\"` thread. The voice model or canvas handles the clarification round-trip before dispatching.\n\n### 2. Project Registry\n\n**Format:** YAML file, read per-invocation by the router\n\nDefines projects the router knows about:\n\n```yaml\nprojects:\n  options-pipeline:\n    aliases: [\"the pipeline\", \"options\"]\n    description: \"Options data pipeline on apexalgo-iad\"\n    cluster: apexalgo-iad\n    namespace: options\n    intent_support: [status, action, brainstorm]\n    workflows:\n      deploy:\n        steps: [ci-status, image-tag, argocd-sync, pod-status]\n\n  ibkr-mcp:\n    aliases: [\"ibkr\", \"the mcp\"]\n    description: \"IBKR MCP server\"\n    cluster: apexalgo-iad\n    namespace: ibkr-mcp\n    intent_support: [status, brainstorm, lookup]\n```\n\nUpdated by the self-modification agent. Hot-reloaded on every router call.\n\n### 3. Fetch Strand\n\n**Runtime:** Deterministic code, no LLM\n\nExecutes a command matrix based on `intent_type` and the project's registry entry. No LLM decisions — the commands are determined by the intent type and project config.\n\nExample command matrix for `intent_type: status`:\n\n| Source | Command |\n|--------|---------|\n| Pod status | `kubectl get pods -n {namespace}` |\n| ArgoCD sync | `curl argocd-ro-api/applications/{app}` |\n| Git log | `git -C {repo_path} log -10 --oneline` |\n| Bead list | `br list --project {slug} --status open` |\n| CI status | `kubectl get workflows -n argo-workflows -l project={slug}` |\n\nResults are structured data passed to the Synthesize strand. Fetch runs each source concurrently; partial results (from sources that respond first) are passed to Synthesize incrementally to support streaming output.\n\nA `fetch_coverage` field tracks which sources succeeded and which failed. Failed sources are surfaced as caveats in the result.\n\n### 4. Synthesize Strand\n\n**Runtime:** Direct API call (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy)\n\nTakes `{intent_spec, fetched_context}` → produces `{data, summary}`.\n\n- `data`: structured JSON matching the component library's expected schema for this intent type\n- `summary`: 1-3 sentence narration suitable for audio mode\n\nSingle-turn inference. Reads its system prompt from a file per invocation (hot-reload). The synthesize prompt is the highest-leverage artifact in the system — it defines result format, detail level, and what fields to include.\n\nRuns once per intent thread in parallel across all active threads.\n\n### 5. Escalate Strand\n\n**Runtime:** Direct API call + `br create`\n\nFor task-profile intents that need durable async handling:\n1. One LLM call to formulate a bead body from the intent + context\n2. `br create` to create the bead in the appropriate project\n3. Returns a pending-card spec with the bead reference\n\nThe bead watcher subsequently bridges bead closure to result delivery.\n\n### 6. Voice Model\n\n**Runtime:** Realtime API (OpenAI or Claude Realtime, persistent session)\n\nThe conversational layer. Receives transcribed utterances (or audio directly), calls `dispatch_intent()` tool to trigger routing, and narrates results at appropriate moments.\n\nTool calls are triggers, not queries. `dispatch_intent()` returns an acknowledgment immediately; results arrive out-of-band and are surfaced by the voice model at natural conversational pauses.\n\nThe voice model's system prompt controls narration style, batching behavior, urgency handling, and multi-turn topic tracking. Read per session-turn from a file (hot-reload).\n\nIn audio mode there is no canvas. The voice model reads `result.summary` directly. When a user transitions from audio to canvas, pending results are rendered using the component library and appear on canvas — session continuity means the canvas catches up.\n\n### 7. UI-Regen Agent\n\n**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n\nSteward of the component library. Given a result's data shape:\n1. Finds the best-fit existing component (semantic match against component descriptions)\n2. If no good fit, generates a new component from scratch and stores it\n3. Applies the component template to result data accounting for layout bucket\n4. On feedback, iterates the component (updates library, triggers canvas re-render)\n\nMulti-step: reads library, generates or selects, writes back. Claude Code's file access and tool use make this the right runtime.\n\nCreated as a task bead; not on the hot path. Canvas updates in place when a component is versioned (SSE push: `component_updated: {component_id, version}`).\n\n### 8. Self-Modification Agent\n\n**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n\nReads and writes the artifacts that encode system behavior:\n- Router prompt (`prompts/router.md`)\n- Project registry (`config/registry.yaml`)\n- Strand prompts (`prompts/fetch.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, etc.)\n- Urgency classifier prompt (`prompts/urgency.md`)\n- Voice model prompt (`prompts/voice.md`)\n- Monitoring config (`config/monitoring.yaml`)\n- Exception routing rules (`config/exceptions.yaml`)\n\nWorkflow for every modification:\n1. Read current artifact\n2. Understand intent of change\n3. Generate updated artifact\n4. Surface diff to user (in canvas or narrated in audio mode)\n5. On approval: write artifact, hot-reload takes effect on next invocation\n6. On rejection: discard\n\nSafety model:\n- Diffs before every application — no silent changes\n- All artifacts versioned; any update can be rolled back with one instruction\n- Confidence threshold: unambiguous changes (adding an alias) can auto-apply with notification; structural changes always require explicit approval\n\n### 9. Background Analysis Bead\n\n**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead, low priority)\n\nRuns on a schedule. Reads intent history and engagement signals from the session store, identifies patterns, proposes artifact updates.\n\nExamples:\n- \"User consistently drills into status results for more detail → propose adding causality fields to synthesize prompt\"\n- \"User ignores pipeline monitoring pushes for 5 days → propose lowering urgency tier\"\n- \"After deploy status, user always asks about pod logs within 2 minutes → propose speculative pre-fetch\"\n\nProposals are surfaced as cards on canvas for user review. Auto-apply only above a high confidence threshold.\n\n### 10. Bead Watcher\n\n**Runtime:** Daemon process, no LLM\n\nWatches for bead close events from NEEDLE workers. On close:\n1. Reads `session_id` from closed bead metadata\n2. Looks up active surface in session store\n3. Writes result to `results` table\n4. Fires SSE push to canvas or Telegram push if no canvas active\n\nPure I/O. Runs as a long-running process (Deployment, not a K8s Job per infrastructure conventions).\n\n---\n\n## Data Model\n\n### Session Store (SQLite)\n\n```sql\nsessions (\n  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  created_at  INTEGER,\n  last_active INTEGER,\n  primary_surface_id TEXT\n)\n\nsurfaces (\n  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  session_id      TEXT,\n  type            TEXT,  -- 'canvas' | 'telegram' | 'audio'\n  state           TEXT,  -- 'active' | 'idle' | 'disconnected'\n  always_available INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- 1 for Telegram\n  last_seen       INTEGER\n)\n\nutterances (\n  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  session_id  TEXT,\n  raw_text    TEXT,\n  created_at  INTEGER\n)\n\nintents (\n  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  utterance_id TEXT,\n  session_id   TEXT,\n  topic_id     TEXT,\n  project_slug TEXT,\n  intent_type  TEXT,\n  status       TEXT,  -- 'pending' | 'dispatched' | 'resolved' | 'cancelled'\n  bead_ref     TEXT,  -- set for task-profile intents\n  created_at   INTEGER,\n  resolved_at  INTEGER\n)\n\nresults (\n  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  intent_id   TEXT,\n  topic_id    TEXT,\n  session_id  TEXT,\n  summary     TEXT,\n  data        TEXT,  -- JSON\n  urgency     TEXT,  -- 'critical' | 'high' | 'normal' | 'low'\n  created_at  INTEGER,\n  surfaced_at INTEGER,\n  acked_at    INTEGER\n)\n\ntopics (\n  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  label        TEXT,\n  type         TEXT,  -- 'project' | 'research' | 'personal' | 'exception' | 'compound'\n  project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n  scope        TEXT,  -- 'session' | 'cross-session' | 'global'\n  session_id   TEXT,  -- null for cross-session/global\n  created_at   INTEGER,\n  last_active  INTEGER,\n  archived_at  INTEGER\n)\n\ntopic_context_cache (\n  topic_id     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  context_data TEXT,  -- JSON: pre-fetched context (kubectl, git, beads results)\n  fetched_at  INTEGER,\n  expires_at  INTEGER\n)\n\nfeedback_signals (\n  signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  signal_type  TEXT,\n  session_id   TEXT,\n  result_id    TEXT,\n  topic_id     TEXT,\n  timestamp    INTEGER,\n  data         TEXT,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n  surface_type TEXT,\n  processed    INTEGER,  -- 0 or 1\n  processed_at INTEGER\n)\n\nintent_topics (\n  intent_id TEXT,\n  topic_id  TEXT,\n  PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id)\n)\n```\n\n### Component Library (SQLite, separate DB)\n\n```sql\ncomponents (\n  id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  name          TEXT,\n  description   TEXT,\n  html_template TEXT,\n  version       INTEGER,\n  created_at    INTEGER,\n  last_used     INTEGER,\n  usage_count   INTEGER\n)\n\ncomponent_versions (\n  component_id  TEXT,\n  version       INTEGER,\n  html_template TEXT,\n  created_at    INTEGER,\n  change_note   TEXT,\n  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, version)\n)\n\ncard_cache (\n  result_id         TEXT,\n  component_id      TEXT,\n  component_version INTEGER,\n  layout_bucket     TEXT,  -- 'compact' | 'normal' | 'expanded'\n  rendered_html     TEXT,\n  created_at        INTEGER,\n  PRIMARY KEY (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n)\n\ncomponent_tags (\n  component_id TEXT,\n  tag TEXT,\n  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, tag)\n)\n\ncomponent_usage_patterns (\n  component_id TEXT,\n  result_type TEXT,  -- e.g., \"pod-status\", \"git-log\"\n  match_score REAL,  -- 0-1\n  sample_count INTEGER,\n  last_matched INTEGER,\n  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, result_type)\n)\n```\n\n---\n\n## File System Layout\n\n```\naide-de-camp/\n├── adc                      ← CLI entry point (shell script or Python package)\n├── config/\n│   ├── registry.yaml        ← project registry (hot-reloaded by router)\n│   ├── monitoring.yaml      ← ambient monitoring rules\n│   └── exceptions.yaml      ← exception routing rules\n├── prompts/\n│   ├── router.md            ← intent segmentation prompt\n│   ├── synthesize.md        ← result generation prompt\n│   ├── voice.md             ← voice model system prompt\n│   ├── urgency.md           ← urgency classification prompt\n│   ├── fetch/               ← per-intent-type fetch instructions\n│   │   ├── status.md\n│   │   ├── action.md\n│   │   └── ...\n│   └── escalate/            ← escalate/task-profile prompts\n│       └── task-profile.md\n├── src/\n│   ├── main.py              ← FastAPI app entry point\n│   ├── registry.py          ← project registry loader\n│   ├── agents/              ← agent implementations\n│   │   ├── self_modification.py  ← self-improvement agent\n│   │   └── ui_regen.py          ← UI-regen agent\n│   ├── components/          ← component library and hot-reload\n│   │   ├── library.py           ← component library DB operations\n│   │   └── hot_reload.py        ← hot-reload watcher\n│   ├── context/             ← context warming and pre-fetch\n│   │   ├── warmer.py            ← context warmer for active topics\n│   │   └── prefetch.py          ← speculative pre-fetch\n│   ├── conversation/        ← multi-turn conversation handling\n│   ├── diff/                ← diff generation for results\n│   ├── environment/         ← environment and repo discovery\n│   │   └── discovery.py        ← repo scanner and registry\n│   ├── escalate/            ← escalate strand for task-profile intents\n│   │   ├── handler.py           ← escalate request handler\n│   │   ├── llm.py               ← LLM calls for bead formulation\n│   │   └── commands.py          ├── bead creation commands\n│   ├── feedback/            ← feedback processing and background analysis\n│   │   ├── processor.py         ← explicit feedback processor\n│   │   ├── signals.py           ← implicit feedback signal tracking\n│   │   └── background_analysis.py ← background analysis bead\n│   ├── fetch/               ← fetch strand (deterministic, per intent type)\n│   │   ├── commands.py          ← fetch command matrix\n│   │   ├── executor.py          ← command executor\n│   │   ├── orchestrator.py      ← parallel fetch orchestration\n│   │   └── strand.py            ← store and extraction\n│   │   ├── store.py             ← memory persistence\n│   │   └── extraction.py        ← memory extraction from results\n│   ├── monitoring/          ← ambient monitoring\n│   │   └── ambient.py           ← ambient monitoring rules\n│   ├── realtime/            ← OpenAI Realtime API voice session\n│   │   ├── session.py           ← voice session handler\n│   │   ├── session/             ← session store (SQLite)\n│   │   └── store.py             ← session store operations\n│   ├── sse/                 ← SSE broadcasting\n│   │   ├── broadcaster.py       ← SSE connection registry\n│   │   └── events.py            ← SSE event types\n│   ├── surface/             ← surface routing\n│   │   └── router.py            ← result surface routing logic\n│   ├── Telegram delivery\n│   ├── topic/               ← topic model\n│   │   └── model.py             ← topic operations\n│   ├── watcher/             ← bead watcher daemon\n│   │   └── daemon.py            ← NEEDLE bead watcher\n│   ├── canvas/              ← web frontend (SSE consumer, card renderer)\n│   │   └── index.html           ← single-page canvas UI\n│   └── cli/                 ← adc CLI\n│       ├── main.py              ← CLI entry point\n│       ├── commands.py          ← CLI commands\n│       ├── config.py            ← CLI configuration\n│       └── sse.py               ← SSE streaming for CLI\n├── data/\n│   ├── session.db           ← session store (SQLite)\n│   └── components.db        ← component library (SQLite)\n└── docs/\n    ├── research/            ← architecture and design research\n    ├── plan/\n    │   └── plan.md          ← this file\n    └── notes/\n        └── naming.md\n```\n\n---\n\n## Deployment Model\n\naide-de-camp is a **live web application**, not a static site. The FastAPI server is the intelligence layer — it handles SSE connections, maintains session state, runs the router and synthesize strands, and serves the frontend HTML. There is no CDN path (no CF Pages).\n\n### Why not static\n\n- SSE connections are long-lived; CDNs cannot proxy them\n- WebSocket for the Realtime API voice session requires a persistent backend\n- The session store (SQLite) and artifact store (prompts, registry) require a writable filesystem\n- Hot-reload depends on the running server reading updated files from disk on each invocation\n\n### Current Deployment: Phase 0 (Hetzner server directly)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\nThe server runs as a process on the Hetzner server itself, not in k8s:\n- NEEDLE workers and the aide-de-camp server share the same filesystem\n- Self-modification agent writes directly to `prompts/` and `config/` — hot-reload works without any coordination\n- Exposed via Tailscale (the server is already on the mesh); no ingress config needed\n- SQLite DBs are local files; no PVC required\n\nRunning command: `uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000`\n\nNo container, no CI, no ArgoCD. Managed as a long-running process (see CLAUDE.md for restart commands).\n\n### Release flow (Phase 0)\n\nVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. No CI build — runs from source.\n\nRelease: `bump version in pyproject.toml` → `commit` → `git tag vX.Y.Z` → `push`.\n\n### Future: Phase 1+ (containerized, ardenone-cluster)\n\n**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n\nOnce session persistence and multi-surface routing are needed, containerize and move to k8s:\n\n```\nDocker image: ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp\nDeployment: ardenone-cluster, namespace: aide-de-camp\nPVC: /data/ (SATA, ReadWriteOnce)\n  /data/session.db        ← session store\n  /data/components.db     ← component library\n  /data/prompts/          ← hot-reloadable prompt files\n  /data/config/           ← registry.yaml, monitoring.yaml, exceptions.yaml\n```\n\nThe artifact store (prompts, registry) moves from the repo's working directory to the PVC. The self-modification agent updates artifacts via the aide-de-camp API (`PATCH /artifacts/{name}`), which writes to the PVC path the server reads from.\n\n### Future: Traefik configuration for SSE and WebSocket (Phase 1+)\n\n**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n\nTraefik's default response buffering breaks SSE. The IngressRoute needs:\n\n```yaml\n# IngressRoute annotations\ntraefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: aide-de-camp-strip-prefix@kubernetescrd\n\n# Middleware: disable buffering, extend timeouts\napiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\nkind: Middleware\nmetadata:\n  name: aide-de-camp-streaming\nspec:\n  buffering:\n    maxResponseBodyBytes: 0   # disable buffering\n  headers:\n    customResponseHeaders:\n      X-Accel-Buffering: \"no\"\n```\n\nServersTransport timeout:\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\nkind: ServersTransport\nmetadata:\n  name: aide-de-camp-longlived\nspec:\n  forwardingTimeouts:\n    responseHeaderTimeout: 0    # no timeout on SSE streams\n    dialTimeout: 5s\n```\n\nDUCK-E's existing WebSocket IngressRoute config is the reference implementation for this cluster.\n\n### SQLite concurrency\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\nMultiple writers (bead watcher, background analysis bead, session handler) require WAL mode to avoid lock contention:\n\n```python\n# On DB open\nconn.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\nconn.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n```\n\nWAL mode allows concurrent reads during writes and serializes multiple writers without blocking. For a personal single-user app, this is sufficient.\n\n### adc CLI connectivity\n\nThe `adc` CLI is a thin HTTP client. It talks to the running server over Tailscale:\n\n```bash\n# ~/.config/adc/config\nserver_url = \"http://localhost:8000\"   # Phase 0 (local)\n# Future: \"http://aide-de-camp.ardenone.com\" (Phase 1+)\n```\n\nNo local inference. The CLI sends requests to the FastAPI backend and streams the SSE response to the terminal.\n\n### Future: CI/CD (Phase 1+)\n\n**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n\nNew Argo WorkflowTemplate `aide-de-camp-build` on iad-ci:\n- Docker build → `ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp`\n- Update image tag in `jedarden/declarative-config` (k8s/ardenone-cluster/aide-de-camp/)\n- ArgoCD syncs automatically\n\nSame pattern as every other containerized service in the stack.\n\n---\n\n## Technology Stack\n\n| Layer | Choice | Reason |\n|-------|--------|--------|\n| Backend | FastAPI (Python) | Reuse DUCK-E scaffolding; SSE + WebSocket support; async-native |\n| STT | Web Speech API → whisper-stt fallback | Zero-latency browser-native; whisper-stt already deployed |\n| Voice session | OpenAI Realtime API (via DUCK-E session handler) | DUCK-E scaffolding already built on this |\n| LLM calls | ZAI proxy (`llm-proxy.ardenone.com`) | Pay-per-token, no subscription pressure on hot path |\n| Task workers | NEEDLE + Claude Code | Existing infrastructure, unchanged |\n| Session store | SQLite (WAL mode) | Lightweight, local, additive-only schema; single-user app |\n| Artifact store | PVC-mounted filesystem (Phase 1+) / local filesystem (Phase 0) | Writable at runtime; hot-reload reads per-invocation |\n| Frontend | Vanilla JS + Web Components | No build pipeline; SSE consumer + client-side card renderer |\n| Phase 0 hosting | Hetzner server, local process | Simplest path; shared filesystem with NEEDLE workers |\n| Phase 1+ hosting | ardenone-cluster, k8s Deployment | Behind existing Traefik + Tailscale ingress |\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Phases\n\n### Phase 0 — Minimal Viable Surface (~2 days)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\n*Verification evidence:* see `docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md` (smoke test results, 20+ runs with all tests passing)\n\nValidates the core question: does routing + parallel dispatch reduce friction?\n\n- Single HTML page: textarea + mic button (Web Speech API)\n- `POST /dispatch` endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream\n- Results appear as basic cards as agents resolve\n- No persistence, no auth, no component library, no ambient push\n\nDeliverable: the core query loop working end-to-end.\n\n### Phase 1 — Session and Topics (~1 week)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\n*Verification evidence:* see `docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md` (session store, SSE, surface registration all verified)\n\nResults persist; the canvas has memory.\n\n- Session store (SQLite, 7+ tables with topic_context_cache and feedback_signals)\n- Topic model: canvas shows one card per active topic, updated in place\n- Telegram surface fallback (reuse telegram-claude-bridge)\n- Bead watcher: closed NEEDLE beads push results to active surface\n- Workload summary on reconnect\n- Staleness indicators on cards\n\nDeliverable: sessions that survive browser refresh; Telegram fallback working.\n\n### Phase 2 — Self-Improvement Loop (~2 weeks)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\n*Verification evidence:* see `docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md` (component library, hot-reload manager verified)\n\nThe interface can be improved by talking into it.\n\n- Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE)\n- Hot-reload confirmed at every artifact layer\n- Component library: UI-regen agent generates first components from actual result shapes\n- Canvas live-updates when a component is versioned\n- Explicit feedback processed: \"always include X\" → prompt updated → hot-reloaded\n\nDeliverable: at least one end-to-end self-modification cycle working (user instructs change, diff surfaced, approved, takes effect without redeploy).\n\n### Phase 3 — Responsiveness (~2-3 weeks)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\n*Verification evidence:* see `docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md` (ambient monitoring, context warmer verified)\n\nThe interface feels alive, not just reactive.\n\n- Ambient monitoring: active topics watched for state changes\n- Diff-aware results: show what changed since last result\n- Pre-warmed context: active topics refreshed in background every N minutes\n- Multi-turn within topic: follow-up questions deepen current topic context\n- Speculative pre-fetch: common follow-up patterns pre-fetched\n- Notification batching in audio mode\n- Implicit feedback signals fed to background analysis bead\n\nDeliverable: monitoring fires unprompted for a watched topic; follow-up questions are visibly faster.\n\n### Phase 4 — Audio Surface (~1-2 weeks)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\n*Verification evidence:* see `README-PHASE4.md` (full voice session implementation with Realtime API) and `docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md`\n\nFull audio mode via Realtime API.\n\n- Realtime API voice session replaces text input path\n- Tool-as-trigger model: `dispatch_intent()` returns ack, result arrives async\n- Urgency-tiered voice narration\n- Audio-to-canvas session continuity\n\nDeliverable: full voice session with canvas catch-up on surface switch.\n\n### Future Work\n\n**Status: NOT STARTED** ❌\n\n*Note: Phases 0-4 (core surface, sessions, self-improvement, responsiveness, and audio) are complete and verified. The following items are potential enhancements beyond Phase 4:*\n\nPotential enhancements beyond Phase 4:\n- Multi-modal input (image processing for UI feedback via Agentation)\n- Advanced topic clustering and auto-archival\n- Cross-session context persistence with summarization\n- Mobile-native surface (iOS/Android app)\n- Collaborative sessions (multi-user shared canvases)\n- Advanced memory extraction with semantic search\n\n---\n\n## Self-Improvement: Hot-Reload Architecture\n\nEvery artifact that encodes behavior is readable, writable, and reloaded per-invocation:\n\n| Behavior | Artifact | Reload point |\n|----------|----------|-------------|\n| Utterance segmentation | `prompts/router.md` | Each router call |\n| Intent→project routing | `config/registry.yaml` | Each router call |\n| Context fetch strategy | `prompts/fetch/{type}.md` | Each fetch invocation |\n| Result format and detail | `prompts/synthesize.md` | Each synthesize call |\n| Voice narration style | `prompts/voice.md` | Each session turn |\n| Urgency classification | `prompts/urgency.md` | Each escalate call |\n| Visual rendering | Component library (DB) | Each card render |\n| Monitoring rules | `config/monitoring.yaml` | Each monitoring tick |\n\nPer-invocation reload is the implementation strategy (read file, check mtime). File watching adds complexity for no benefit — prompts change rarely and file reads are cheap.\n\n---\n\n## Surface Routing Rules\n\nWhen a result is ready to surface, the bead watcher or synthesize strand uses this priority:\n\n1. The surface the utterance originated from (if still connected)\n2. Most recently active connected surface\n3. Any connected surface\n4. Telegram (always-available fallback)\n\nException-class results (urgency: critical, type: exception) push to Telegram regardless of canvas state if no canvas has been active within the past N minutes.\n\n---\n\n## Relationship to Existing Infrastructure\n\naide-de-camp adds a routing and rendering layer on top of existing infrastructure without replacing any of it:\n\n- **NEEDLE workers** — unchanged. Task beads work exactly as before. The bead watcher is a new daemon that observes bead closure events.\n- **telegram-claude-bridge** — reused for Telegram surface and HUMAN bead push delivery.\n- **whisper-stt** — already deployed on ardenone-cluster; available as STT backend.\n- **ZAI proxy** — all direct API calls (router, synthesize, escalate) route through `llm-proxy.ardenone.com`.\n- **claude-governor** — governs subscription token consumption for self-modification and UI-regen task beads.\n- **DUCK-E** — FastAPI scaffolding, WebSocket handling, OpenAI Realtime API session management, and middleware reused as the voice layer foundation.\n- **beads (br CLI)** — task work items and HUMAN exceptions only. Conversational session state lives in a separate SQLite session store.\n- **kubectl proxies** — fetch strand uses existing kubectl proxy access per cluster.\n- **ArgoCD read-only proxy** — fetch strand reads ArgoCD application state via `argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444`.\n\nNet-new code: aide-de-camp is a substantial implementation spanning multiple subsystems. The current codebase contains approximately **15,400 lines** of Python code across 80+ modules. See the File System Layout section for the full module breakdown.\n\n---\n\n## Security Model\n\n- aide-de-camp runs on the Hetzner server; all cluster access via read-only kubectl proxies (same as existing tooling)\n- No cluster credentials stored by aide-de-camp; existing proxy infrastructure holds them\n- Self-modification agent writes only to the `prompts/` and `config/` directories within the repo\n- All artifact changes go through diff-review before application\n- Rollback available for any artifact via version history\n\n---\n\n## Open Questions\n\n1. **Routing accuracy** — how reliably can a haiku-class model split a rambling multi-project utterance into clean tagged threads? What's the false-split / under-split rate in practice?\n\n2. **Context latency** — can the fetch strand reliably hit kubectl + ArgoCD + git + beads within a 2-3s window? Which sources need timeouts and what's the degraded-result behavior?\n\n3. **Component selection** — when does the UI-regen agent generate a new component vs. stretch an existing one? How is \"good enough\" match defined?\n\n4. **Concurrency budget** — how many parallel synthesize calls can the ZAI proxy handle without queue pressure affecting the <3s target?\n\n5. **Topic vague reference resolution** — \"the pipeline\" vs. \"options pipeline\" vs. \"options-pipeline\". When does the router resolve from context vs. ask for clarification?\n\n6. **Voice UX** — push-to-talk vs. continuous listening + VAD silence detection. What's the right default for the audio surface?\n\n7. **Disambiguation flow** — when router confidence is below threshold, how does the clarification round-trip work without breaking conversational flow in audio mode?\n","numLines":811,"startLine":1,"totalLines":811}}}

Create Bead (br CLI)\n75\t\n76\t**Command:**\n77\t\n78\t```bash\n79\tbr create \\\n80\t  --title \"[project-slug] task-verb...\" \\\n81\t  --type task \\\n82\t  --metadata '{\"session_id\":\"...\",\"intent_id\":\"...\",\"origin_surface_id\":\"...\"}'\n83\t```\n84\t\n85\t**Bead metadata includes:**\n86\t- `session_id`: For bead watcher routing\n87\t- `intent_id`: Links bead to originating intent\n88\t- `origin_surface_id`: Which surface originated the request\n89\t- `project_slug`: For project workspace routing\n90\t- `topic_id`: If continuing an existing topic\n91\t\n92\t### 3. Return Pending-Card Spec\n93\t\n94\t```json\n95\t{\n96\t  \"type\": \"pending\",\n97\t  \"id\": \"pending-{bead_id}\",\n98\t  \"intent_id\": \"{intent_id}\",\n99\t  \"bead_id\": \"{bead_id}\",\n100\t  \"title\": \"[Short title from utterance]\",\n101\t  \"summary\": \"Working on: {utterance preview}\",\n102\t  \"status\": \"pending\",\n103\t  \"urgency\": \"normal|high|critical\",\n104\t  \"created_at\": 1234567890,\n105\t  \"metadata\": {\n106\t    \"project_slug\": \"...\",\n107\t    \"topic_id\": \"...\",\n108\t    \"bead_type\": \"task\"\n109\t  }\n110\t}\n111\t```\n112\t\n113\t## Bead Watcher Integration\n114\t\n115\tOnce created, the bead watcher monitors for bead closure. When the bead is closed:\n116\t\n117\t1. Bead watcher receives the closure event\n118\t2. Reads bead body and extracts completion summary\n119\t3. Routes result to the originating session via SSE\n120\t4. Surface updates from pending-card to result-card\n121\t\n122\t## Routing Decision\n123\t\n124\tThe intent router classifies task-profile intents when:\n125\t- Intent requires multi-step work\n126\t- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\")\n127\t- Intent complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n128\t- Request involves implementation work\n129\t\n130\t**Classification criteria:**\n131\t- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\"\n132\t- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"refactor\", \"optimize\"\n133\t- Time indicators: \"when you get a chance\", \"eventually\"\n134\t\n135\t## Fallback Behavior\n136\t\n137\t- **LLM timeout**: Retry once, then escalate with simplified bead body\n138\t- **br create failure**: Return error to user, suggest manual bead creation\n139\t- **Missing project**: Ask user to specify project context\n140\t\n141\t## Context Sources\n142\t\n143\tFor escalation, include these context fields if available:\n144\t\n145\t- **Git status**: Current branch, uncommitted changes\n146\t- **Cluster state**: Relevant pod/deployment status\n147\t- **CI status**: Recent workflow runs\n148\t- **Existing beads**: Related open beads for the project\n149\t- **Topic context**: Previous turns in this topic\n150\t\n151\tThe escalate strand is the only strand that creates durable work items. All other strands are read-only or transient actions.\n152\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"acc9c52a-de90-4405-90c6-fa2ea26e7b32","uuid":"472574a2-6487-4ddf-a7af-2d33639511b8","timestamp":"2026-07-03T01:00:27.553Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/escalate/task-profile.md","content":"# Escalate Strand: Task-Profile Intent\n\nThis document defines the escalation strategy for `intent_type: task-profile` intents that require durable async handling via NEEDLE beads.\n\n## What We Escalate\n\nTask-profile intents are user requests that:\n- Require extended work beyond a single-turn response\n- Need durable tracking (survives session restart)\n- Benefit from structured Claude Code execution\n- May require multiple steps or research\n\nExamples:\n- \"Add authentication to the Kalshi tape service\"\n- \"Investigate why the pipeline is slow\"\n- \"Set up monitoring for the new deployment\"\n\n## Escalation Flow\n\n### 1. Formulate Bead Body (LLM Call)\n\n**Model:** Claude Sonnet 4 (via ZAI proxy)\n**Timeout:** 30 seconds\n\nGiven the user's intent and available context, produce a well-structured bead body that:\n\n1. **Captures the full scope** of the request\n2. **Includes relevant context** (project, cluster, specific resources)\n3. **Defines clear success criteria**\n4. **Structures the work** for Claude Code execution\n\n**System Prompt:**\n\n```\nYou are ADC's escalate handler. Your job is to formulate a clear, actionable NEEDLE bead body from a user's intent.\n\nA NEEDLE bead is a task work item with:\n- A clear title describing what needs to be done\n- A detailed body with context, requirements, and success criteria\n- Proper structure for Claude Code to execute\n\nGiven the user's intent and any available context, produce a bead body that:\n1. Captures the full scope of the request\n2. Includes relevant context (project, cluster, specific resources)\n3. Defines clear success criteria\n4. Structures the work for Claude Code execution\n\nOutput ONLY the bead body as markdown. Do not include explanations or meta-commentary.\n```\n\n**Input Context:**\n- User utterance (verbatim)\n- Intent type\n- Project slug (if applicable)\n- Topic ID (if continuing an existing topic)\n- Additional context (git status, cluster state, etc.)\n\n**Output:**\n\n```markdown\n## Task\n[Clear, actionable description of what needs to be done]\n\n## Context\n[Relevant context about the project, cluster, resources]\n\n## Success Criteria\n[Specific, measurable criteria for completion]\n\n## Implementation Notes\n[Any technical details, constraints, or guidance for Claude Code]\n```\n\n### 2. Create Bead (br CLI)\n\n**Command:**\n\n```bash\nbr create \\\n  --title \"[project-slug] task-verb...\" \\\n  --type task \\\n  --metadata '{\"session_id\":\"...\",\"intent_id\":\"...\",\"origin_surface_id\":\"...\"}'\n```\n\n**Bead metadata includes:**\n- `session_id`: For bead watcher routing\n- `intent_id`: Links bead to originating intent\n- `origin_surface_id`: Which surface originated the request\n- `project_slug`: For project workspace routing\n- `topic_id`: If continuing an existing topic\n\n### 3. Return Pending-Card Spec\n\n```json\n{\n  \"type\": \"pending\",\n  \"id\": \"pending-{bead_id}\",\n  \"intent_id\": \"{intent_id}\",\n  \"bead_id\": \"{bead_id}\",\n  \"title\": \"[Short title from utterance]\",\n  \"summary\": \"Working on: {utterance preview}\",\n  \"status\": \"pending\",\n  \"urgency\": \"normal|high|critical\",\n  \"created_at\": 1234567890,\n  \"metadata\": {\n    \"project_slug\": \"...\",\n    \"topic_id\": \"...\",\n    \"bead_type\": \"task\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Bead Watcher Integration\n\nOnce created, the bead watcher monitors for bead closure. When the bead is closed:\n\n1. Bead watcher receives the closure event\n2. Reads bead body and extracts completion summary\n3. Routes result to the originating session via SSE\n4. Surface updates from pending-card to result-card\n\n## Routing Decision\n\nThe intent router classifies task-profile intents when:\n- Intent requires multi-step work\n- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\")\n- Intent complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n- Request involves implementation work\n\n**Classification criteria:**\n- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\"\n- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"refactor\", \"optimize\"\n- Time indicators: \"when you get a chance\", \"eventually\"\n\n## Fallback Behavior\n\n- **LLM timeout**: Retry once, then escalate with simplified bead body\n- **br create failure**: Return error to user, suggest manual bead creation\n- **Missing project**: Ask user to specify project context\n\n## Context Sources\n\nFor escalation, include these context fields if available:\n\n- **Git status**: Current branch, uncommitted changes\n- **Cluster state**: Relevant pod/deployment status\n- **CI status**: Recent workflow runs\n- **Existing beads**: Related open beads for the project\n- **Topic context**: Previous turns in this topic\n\nThe escalate strand is the only strand that creates durable work items. All other strands are read-only or transient actions.\n","numLines":152,"startLine":1,"totalLines":152}}}
